content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: File

Date: December 15, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Ted Prim

Subject: Interpretation of 18700(d)(2) and (f)(1)

This provision permits a disqualified official to appear before his agency in at least two contexts:

1. If the official is disqualified from participating in a matter at a public meeting, he may address the body from the audience as a member of the general public.

2. In the case where there is no public meeting but private contacts are customary by members of the public, the disqualified official also may make such contacts; e.g., applying for a building or similar permit.

This provision does not permit the official to represent interests other than those which are solely personal. Therefore, he may not represent clients even from the audience of a public meeting. This provision also does not permit the official to expressly or impliedly coerce officials of his agency during a public or private appearance on matters related solely to his own personal financial interests. Obviously, members of the agency will be mindful of an official's rank when he appears before them as a member of the general public so that some implicit coercion may always exist. However, no other coercion is permitted under the regulation.

TP:nrw
